title: Disable automatic hyperlinks when writing URLs in updates
excerpt:
I frequently need to share URLs with other people. I create a new Update on an Item and paste the raw URL that I want to share. But I find that URLs oftentimes get “over-processed” by Monday.

posts.author: MikeInSaaS (Mike)
posts.cookedHtml:
I frequently need to share URLs with other people. I create a new Update on an Item and paste the raw URL that I want to share. But I find that URLs oftentimes get “over-processed” by Monday.

To show you what I mean, let’s take this URL for example:

https://bibsys-almaprimo.hosted.exlibrisgroup.com/primo-explore/fulldisplay?docid=TN_cdi_proquest_journals_2428367506&context=PC&vid=DIAKON&lang=no_NO&search_scope=default_scope&adaptor=primo_central_multiple_fe&tab=default_tab&query=any,contains,Conceptions%20and%20Practices%20of%20Critical%20Thinking%20in%20Chinese%20Schools:%20An%20Example%20from%20Shanghai&mode=Basic

When I write a new Update and paste that raw URL from my computer’s clipboard, look at what Monday does to it:

pasted link707×226 20 KB

Part of the URL gets truncated and hyperlinked. I don’t want either of those things to happen. I just want to share the URL exactly as I pasted it.

It was probably intended as a feature, but realistically it interferes with my work like a bug. Is there any way to stop Monday from automatically altering the URLs that I write in plain text?

It doesn’t always need to happen automatically. If I wanted a hyperlink, I would paste one, or I would use the ‘Link’ button in the formatting menu:

link button695×213 19.8 KB

Thanks!

posts.author: clessard (Cameron Lessard)
posts.cookedHtml: I have seen this too! Big enhancement. Thanks for posting.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @MikeInSaaS

Welcome to the community. It’s a very old discussion whether you need to encode comma’s inside your URL. Try a Google search on “do i need to encode comma in url” and you see why this is happening. Your URL contains unencoded comma’s.

posts.author: mrdb (Dean Baker)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey Mike, I just use the text formatting option and choose “Code”.

posts.author: tommemben (Tom Charde)
posts.cookedHtml: Any update on this? We have tried everything, but Monday always truncates the URLs (and sometimes cuts off the “http://”), and converts them to hyperlinks – all of which make it impossible to document URLs for other team members to be able to pull from. It’s maddening.

posts.author: rturnage (Robert Turnage)
posts.cookedHtml: Even using Code formatting, Monday is converting URLs to hyperlinks AND dropping the https, breaking the javascript code I’m trying to share with a developer.

posts.author: MikeInSaaS (Mike)
posts.cookedHtml:
Any update here? When trying to share metadata, an identifier like “http://www.google.com/” is a VERY different thing than “google.com”, and when customers receive erroneous information because Monday.com manipulates what we write, it cases failures. This is a dangerous bug.

It even applies to the “code” formatting, so there’s no way to avoid it. Monday.com does not allow information to be posted as written. Everything is subjected to an unstoppable autocorrect. It’s infuriating. Please help.

posts.author: mmcclellan (Megan)
posts.cookedHtml: Just wanted to bump to say that 2.5 years later, this is still an issue. Even when code formatting is selected, urls and srcs in embeds get rewritten, causing errors if developers try to implement the code passed to them through Monday.
